findings.message: Phase 3 notes 'code intact' for recovered interrupted session but provides no verification method or commit hash.

Line 9 contains a literal shell expression `$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")` that was never substituted. The dispatch log shows a template artifact instead of an actual timestamp for the kr62ia executor dispatch date.
'consider' used 3 times in task items.
'consider' used twice as task instruction.
'consider' used 4 times as an action verb in task items. Should be replaced with imperative verbs: 'implement', 'add', 'refactor', 'extract'.
'appropriate' used once; 'consider' once.
'supplemented' and 'consider' are vague instruction verbs.
'richer implementations' is subjective.
'Proportionality applies' is contextual but slightly vague.
'proactively suggest a version bump' — 'proactively' is vague. Should specify a trigger condition.

Change Enforcement Rules reference files in peer repos (../openpeon/, ../homebrew-tap/, peonping-x-bot) that are not part of this repository. An AI agent working from a fresh clone cannot verify these. Rules are correct for human developers but create a verification gap for automated review.

Variables rgb_r, rgb_g, rgb_b, dismiss_secs, icon_win_path, y_offset interpolated directly into PowerShell -Command string in WSL popup path. icon_win_path derives from wslpath which could return paths with characters that break the PowerShell string literal.
Same pattern as WSL path (finding #3) but for MSYS2. toast_xml_win from cygpath interpolated into PowerShell command string.

Registry JSON fetched from peonping.github.io controls source_repo, source_ref, and source_path for all pack downloads. A compromised registry or MITM could redirect downloads to attacker-controlled GitHub repos. Input validation (is_safe_source_repo) limits but does not eliminate risk.

Sound files are downloaded via curl and checksums are stored, but checksums are only verified on subsequent cache hits, not on the initial download. A tampered file downloaded for the first time would pass silently.

py_path result interpolated via single-quote wrapping in python3 -c '...' string. Single quotes in the path (possible on some systems) would break the Python string literal. The is_safe_filename validator uses a permissive charset that allows special characters.

FALLBACK_REPO is hardcoded to 'PeonPing/og-packs' with no override mechanism. If the PeonPing GitHub org were compromised, all installs using the fallback path would silently download from the compromised repo.

Sends category name to localhost:19998 relay without authentication. Currently safe as category is derived from a hardcoded case statement. If the relay endpoint or category mapping were ever extended to include user input, this becomes a SSRF/injection vector.
skill sets user_invocable: false but CLAUDE.md documents `/peon-ping-config` as a user-accessible slash command. This contradiction may prevent the skill from appearing in autocomplete.
Skill file has no explicit Output Format section. The document describes behavior and examples but does not specify what the assistant should output after completing the configuration change.
Frontmatter does not include a model field.
SKILL.md sets user_invocable: false but CLAUDE.md section 'Skills' lists /peon-ping-config as a user-accessible slash command. Either the frontmatter is wrong or CLAUDE.md is wrong.

Uses hardcoded `~/.claude/hooks/peon-ping/peon.sh` instead of `${CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.claude}`. All other skills (peon-ping-toggle, peon-ping-use, peon-ping-config, peon-ping-rename) use the config dir pattern. This breaks for users with non-default CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR, including some Cursor setups.
Skill file has no explicit Output Format section.
Frontmatter does not include a model field.
'ensure' used as an instruction verb.
peon-ping-log uses ~/.claude/hooks/peon-ping/peon.sh while all other skills use ${CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.claude}. Creates silent failures for users with non-default config directories.
Skill file has no explicit Output Format section documenting what the assistant should return after the rename succeeds or fails.
Frontmatter does not include a model field. The agent model cannot be validated or pinned.
Skill file has no explicit Output Format section. Reports two possible outputs in prose but does not use a structured Output heading.
Frontmatter does not include a model field.

Line 9 contains a literal shell expression `$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")` that was never substituted. The dispatch log shows a template artifact instead of an actual timestamp for the kr62ia executor dispatch date.
